latin: Medicus in aeroplano aegrotum inspiciebat. mulier dixit: 'certe auxilium ferre potes.' medicus respondit: 'possum. et noli me Serliam vocare.'

questions.prompt: Where is this scene taking place?
What is the joke in the doctor's response?
questions.target: He takes 'Shirley' as his name instead of 'surely'
choices: He is not actually a doctor
He takes 'Shirley' as his name instead of 'surely'

translation: A doctor on an airplane was examining a sick person. A woman said: 'Surely you can help.' The doctor responded: 'I can. And don't call me Shirley.'
